steps.title: Build the line from the intercept and one point
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is $(0,-16)$, and the table gives the point $(-12,44)$. The slope is $$m=\frac{44-(-16)}{-12-0}=\frac{60}{-12}=-5.$$ So the line is $y=-5x-16$.

steps.title: Find a and b from the line
steps.content: For $a$, the table row has $y=14$, so solve $14=-5a-16$. This gives $30=-5a$, so $a=-6$. For $b$, use the row where $x=2$: $$b=-5(2)-16=-26.$$
